name: drugood-suite

env_axis.name: split-axis
env_axis.shift_mechanism: 5 domain split axes × LBAP/SBAP variants = 96 configurations from ChEMBL ~2.1M activity records

schema.suite_note: suite — 96 ChEMBL configurations. drugood-lbap-ic50-assay broken out as a core member within the suite.
sub_datasets: 96 configurations parameterized by (split_axis × LBAP/SBAP × specific measurement)

facts: 96 ChEMBL configurations from ~2.1M activity records; 5 domain split axes (assay/scaffold/size/protein/protein-family); LBAP/SBAP variants; tencent-ailab/DrugOOD.
hypotheses: The 5 axes are not statistically independent in ChEMBL but correlate, with scaffold-shift approximating size-shift because molecule size correlates with scaffold complexity.
DrugOOD performance predicts only computational held-out-ChEMBL generalization, not wet-lab generalization (the actual drug-discovery objective).
intuitions: A cross-DrugOOD-TDC scaffold-split agreement check is feasible since both use ChEMBL, and the splits probably disagree in part.
Critiques of the pinned ChEMBL snapshot version likely exist in the literature.
unknowns: Whether assay-as-env captures the right granularity (ID vs family vs protocol) cannot be decided — assays drift in protocol within ChEMBL itself.
Whether the 96 configurations are representative or curated for clear shift cannot be determined; they cover only a small region of (5-axis × ChEMBL) space.
epistemic_state.boundary: ChEMBL-bounded (no patents, no other DBs); the 5 axes are pre-specified, not discovered.

materialization_status: ChEMBL 29 SQLite (4 GB) materialized — the upstream source DrugOOD applies its preprocessing to. DrugOOD config files in repo (/tmp/drugood). For specific OOD configurations (assay/scaffold/size split), apply DrugOOD's curators per their preprocessing scripts.
